ext.description: Rich GUI for Claude Code: context, cache, cost and account-limit transparency, inline diffs, plan mode and checkpoints — running through the Claude Code CLI.
view.cockpit.name: Cockpit
cfg.claudePath: Path to the Claude Code CLI executable.
cfg.language: UI language. 'auto' follows the VS Code language; falls back to English.
cfg.permissionMode: Default permission mode passed to the CLI. Reflected (pre-selected) in the Permission dropdown in the panel; change it there to override just the current session.
cfg.allowAgents: Allow the agent to spawn subagents (Task) and workflows (Workflow). Default off — these run extra parallel agents and can burn a lot of tokens, so by default the CLI starts with --disallowedTools Task,Workflow. Turn on to allow them. Reflected in the 'Agents & workflows' checkbox in the panel; toggle there to override just the current session.
cfg.notifyOnComplete: Show a notification when Claude finishes a turn and the Cockpit panel is not visible.
cfg.showThinking: Expand Claude's thinking blocks by default in the chat.
cfg.spellCheck: Check spelling as you type in the composer, underlining misspelled words. Off by default.
cfg.expandToolCards: Expand tool call cards by default in the timeline.
cfg.userName: Name shown for your messages instead of 'You'. Empty = your OS username.
cfg.ffmpegPath: Path to the ffmpeg executable used for voice dictation capture. Empty = use 'ffmpeg' from PATH.
cfg.voiceLanguage: Language for voice dictation (speech-to-text). Empty = follow the Cockpit UI language.
cfg.voiceCorrect: After stopping dictation, send the text to the internal model for spelling/grammar correction. Clean isolated call (only instruction + text; no tools/MCP/context).
cfg.debugLog: Write detailed diagnostic logs to the "Tootega Cockpit" output channel (cache keep-alive, stats persistence, cache reset/compaction, session lifecycle). Off by default to avoid noise.
cfg.otelEnabled: Start a local OpenTelemetry receiver (127.0.0.1) and turn on the Claude CLI telemetry export so the Usage panel can show lines of code, sessions, commits and edit-tool decisions per model. Opt-in, off by default. Loopback traffic only; never logs credentials or response text.
cfg.daseEnabled: Allow connecting the DASE ORM Designer as an MCP server. When on, a per-session toggle (DASE) appears in the panel; turn it on only while designing a .dsorm model so its tools don't add context weight to other sessions. Requires the DASE extension's MCP server to be running (dase.mcp.enabled).
cfg.daseModel: Model to run sessions on while the DASE integration is enabled for that tab. Empty = keep the session's current model. Use a cheaper/dedicated model for ORM work to save tokens.
cfg.internalModel: Model used for Cockpit's internal AI calls (dictation correction, slash-command help). These are clean, isolated calls (no tools/MCP/context). Haiku is fastest/cheapest.
cfg.verbosity: How much of the timeline to show (display only — does not change the agent). Verbose = everything; Necessary = edits + final explanations; Dialogo = edits + what it's doing; Quiet = final explanations only.
cfg.model: Default model for new sessions. 'Account default' lets the CLI choose. This is reflected (pre-selected) in the Model dropdown in the panel; change it there to override just the current session.
cfg.effort: Default reasoning effort for new sessions. 'CLI default' uses your ~/.claude/settings.json effortLevel. Reflected (pre-selected) in the Effort dropdown in the panel; change it there to override just the current session.
cfg.apiKey: Optional Anthropic API key, used **only** to list available models via `/v1/models`. Leave empty if Claude Code is authenticated by subscription — the model selector falls back to aliases, the active model and a custom field. The key is not used for chat (that always goes through the CLI).
cfg.autoResumeLastSession: When opening a folder, automatically resume the most recent session for that folder (loads its history). Turn off to always start empty.
cfg.autosave: Auto-save an open file before Claude reads or writes it, so the agent works on the latest content.
